title: The Pact
desc: Van Bloot leads you into the depths beneath Gothos Tower to harvest the alien growths.
chainDesc: Track an unknown threat across the UnderWorld alongside Chaor himself.

steps.text:
Chaor is gone. He left Gothos Tower in a column of fire, and you could feel the betrayal radiating off him hotter than the flames. You chose your side.

Van Bloot watches the smoke clear with undisguised satisfaction.

"Smart choice, human. Chaor would have destroyed everything down here and learned nothing. That's always been his weakness — he treats every problem like a nail."

He leads you deeper into the tower, through passages you're certain aren't on any map. The alien growths here are undisturbed — older, more developed. Your scanner picks up patterns in the tendrils that look almost like circuitry.

"I've been siphoning energy from these for months," Van Bloot says, running a claw along one of the pulsing veins. "Mugic amplification. Power storage. Whatever this organism is, it's the most efficient energy conductor I've ever encountered."

He stops at a sealed door. Behind it, your scanner shows a massive concentration of the alien signal.

options.label: Scan the sealed chamber in detail
Ask Van Bloot what's behind the door

steps.text:
Van Bloot opens the door. Inside is a laboratory — crude by human standards, but unmistakably a research facility. Harvested fragments of the alien material sit in containment vessels, pulsing with that deep-ocean glow. Equipment lines the walls, connected to the growths by cables that look half-organic.

"My researchers have been studying these for weeks. We've learned more from careful observation than Chaor learned from punching them." Van Bloot picks up a fragment. It pulses brighter in his hand. "This material responds to Mugic frequencies. It amplifies them. Chaor's brute force couldn't destroy the nest at the Colosseum because the growths were feeding off his own fire energy."

Your scanner confirms it — the fragments in the lab are absorbing ambient energy and re-emitting it in structured patterns.

Then your scanner catches something else. The same flickering shape from before — the creature. But this time, it's not running. It's watching. From inside the largest containment vessel.

Van Bloot follows your gaze and smiles.

"Ah. You've noticed our guest. It arrived three days ago. Walked right into the containment field like it wanted to be found." His eyes narrow with hunger. "Imagine what we could learn from a living specimen."

The creature's bioluminescent markings pulse once. Your scanner registers something that looks almost like... a signal. Directed at you.

options.label: Help Van Bloot study the creature
Record the creature's signal pattern for your own archives
